highlights.title: Ensure pump limits match user settings on startup
changes: Trio updates the pump manager's maximum basal and bolus limits to match your app settings when it starts.
This prevents temporary basal deliveries from being silently rejected due to stale limits.
The app version is bumped to 0.8.3.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1219: Trio v0.8.3 Hotfix: Fix Delivery Limit Regression on Silent PumpManagerRawState Migration
hotfix: ensure correct pump manager delivery limits

highlights.title: Show adjusted ISF in Nightscout determinations
changes: Nightscout determinations now show the adjusted insulin sensitivity (ISF) value instead of 0.
The reported ISF matches the adjusted sensitivity used when deciding basal changes.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1222: Report adjusted ISF in determination output

highlights.title: Fix for pump delivery-limit regression
changes: Fixes a regression that could incorrectly apply delivery limits during silent pump state migrations.
The v0.8.3 hotfix was merged back into development so dev builds include this fix.

sources.title: gordolio/Trio#1224: Trio v0.8.3 Hotfix — sync to dev
Merge branch 'dev' of github.com:nightscout/Trio into hotfix/delivery-limits

items.title: Remove unused glucose conversion function
changes: The internal, unused glucose conversion function was removed from the dosing logic.
Reason strings now show rounded glucose numbers directly instead of calling the converter.
Glucose units and how glucose values are handled remain unchanged.
This is an internal cleanup and should not affect dosing decisions or app behavior.

includedPullRequests.title: Report adjusted ISF in determination output
includedPullRequests.body:
The Swift oref left `isf` as nil in the determination, so the ISF field uploaded to Nightscout came out as 0. This sets it to the adjusted sensitivity, the same value already shown in the reason string.

Dan found the fix, I'm just the secretary.

includedPullRequests.title: Report adjusted ISF in determination output
includedPullRequests.body:
The Swift oref left `isf` as nil in the determination, so the ISF field uploaded to Nightscout came out as 0. This sets it to the adjusted sensitivity, the same value already shown in the reason string.

Dan found the fix, I'm just the secretary.

includedPullRequests.title: Remove unused convertGlucose function
includedPullRequests.body:
This PR removes an unused function from the core determine basal logic in oref-swift: `convertGlucose`. In the original JS implementation, several output properties and reason string values are run through a `convert_bg` function that converts the glucose value to `mmol` based on the `profile.outUnits` value.

However, `profile.outUnits` is always in `.mgdl`, and should always be in `.mgdl` so we should remove this function. The path goes:
- The convert function branches based on the value of `profile.outUnits`
- `profile.outUnits` is set from `bgTarget.userPreferredUnits`
- all of the write paths for the file that saves the `bgTarget` value store these values as mg/dl and hard code `userPreferredUnits` to `.mgdl`.

Unit tests still pass with this change.

includedPullRequests.title: Remove unused convertGlucose function
includedPullRequests.body:
This PR removes an unused function from the core determine basal logic in oref-swift: `convertGlucose`. In the original JS implementation, several output properties and reason string values are run through a `convert_bg` function that converts the glucose value to `mmol` based on the `profile.outUnits` value.

However, `profile.outUnits` is always in `.mgdl`, and should always be in `.mgdl` so we should remove this function. The path goes:
- The convert function branches based on the value of `profile.outUnits`
- `profile.outUnits` is set from `bgTarget.userPreferredUnits`
- all of the write paths for the file that saves the `bgTarget` value store these values as mg/dl and hard code `userPreferredUnits` to `.mgdl`.

Unit tests still pass with this change.

includedPullRequests.title: Trio v0.8.3 Hotfix — sync to dev
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary

Syncs the v0.8.3 patch release back to `dev`. Replacement for #1220 (couldn't push to the head fork — `maintainerCanModify` was off).

Brings:
- #1219 — Fix delivery-limit regression on silent PumpManagerRawState migration (AndreasStokholm)
- Release bookkeeping for v0.8.3

Same content as #1220 — single merge commit `f3463551d` reconciling `main` (post-0.8.3 release) back into `dev`.
